news.news: Serum Institute of India (SII) and the Drugs for Neglected Diseases initiative (DNDi) signed a licensing agreement to advance a monoclonal antibody treatment (VIS513) for dengue. A Phase III clinical trial is scheduled to begin in early 2027 across Brazil, Malaysia, and Thailand, involving 1,000 participants to test the safety and efficacy of the therapy.

Oxford Nanopore Technologies highlighted new FDA draft guidance recommending long-read sequencing for the safety assessment of genome-edited therapies. The guidance emphasizes the need to detect large structural variants and complex on- and off-target editing events that short-read sequencing may miss, positioning nanopore technology as a critical tool for insertion site analysis in cell and gene therapy quality control.
